<mxCell id="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-7" value="TODO which accounts should which users be able to see? is it related to all those in the current org? or is it that if you are an account manager, you can see all accounts? no that ain't right because I don't want Joe Bloggs to see all my abstratium employees.&nbsp; Currently it's implemented that it fetches users that have roles in the clients that i have access too, but that is total BS." style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=center;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects </mxGeometry> </mxCell> <mxCell id="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-9" value="TODO this is a functional question that i have to specify the answer to. built the solution based on those specs.<div><br></div><div>- ADMIN can see everyone. that's why no one gets it.</div><div>- an org owner can see all the accounts that are members in the given org.</div><div>- an org member can see other accounts in the org.</div><div>- the role "abstratium-abstrauth_manage-accounts' is given to anyone, but they can only manage accounts that have membership to their org.</div><div>- an org owner can add anyone to their org</div><div>- but then we have a security issue, since Joe Bloggs can add abstratium.dev@gmail.com to their org, and they would be able to manage my roles in abstrauth!</div><div>- equally, Joe Bloggs must be able to give anyone the role "abstratium-abstratuth_manage-accounts" within their org.</div><div>- is it as simple as the client abstratium-abstrauth is super special? i don't think so because someone might build an app for which they let anyone manage the roles for it, inside abstrauth.</div>" cannot authenticate with foreign clients, since they don't know the client secrets." style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=center;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects <mxCell id="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-42" value="So which accounts does he see? All those in his org, since he is an account manager in his org." style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=center;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;" vertex="1" parent="1"> <mxGeometry x="1180" y="-30" width="190" height="80" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-43" style="rounded=0;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;orthogonalLoop=1;jettySize=auto;html=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;dashed=1;endArrow=none;startFill=1;startArrow=classic;" edge="1" parent="1" source="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-42" target="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-33"> <mxGeometry relative="1" as="geometry"> <mxPoint x="1570" y="220" as="targetPoint" /> <mxPoint x="1636" y="160" as="sourcePoint" /> </mxGeometry> </mxCell> <mxCell id="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-44" value="TODO how does Joe add more account managers for his org? He has to be able to manage the abstrauth client! well he can see the accounts, and then add a role. The ABAC check needs to be: if client is public and user has "manage clients" role, then they can add the role. It is added using the default orgId&nbsp; and is loaded whenever a user signs in." style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=center;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;fillColor=#f8cecc;strokeColor=#b85450;" vertex="1" parent="1"> <mxGeometry x="1385" y="-120" width="310" height="140" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-45" value="TODO can this be abused? Can such a user then add themselves as admin?&nbsp; Yes they can!!!&nbsp; But not if they can only use the roles that are in the list of allowed roles!! Aahhh that is what is missing - making abstrauth public, and adding allowed roles and adding a subscription!!" style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=center;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;fillColor=#f8cecc;strokeColor=#b85450;" vertex="1" parent="1"> <mxGeometry x="1770" y="-120" width="310" height="140" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-46" value="TODO Joe might be an account manager, but he can only manage accounts that are in his org.<div><br></div><div>Ah wait, so we need AccountRole per Org so that if my account is also in his org, i can be a non-manager there, but a manager in mine? probably!</div><div><br></div><div>so all org owners are account managers by default.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>WHEN A USER REGISTERS / IS REGISTERED, always give them the user role to abstrauth in the org of the user who is adding them.&nbsp;</div><div>WHEN THE TOKEN IS CREATED, only read for the orgId for which the token is being created.</div>" style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=center;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;fillColor=#f8cecc;strokeColor=#b85450;" vertex="1" parent="1"> <mxGeometry x="1830" y="40" width="540" height="180" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-47" value="Which OAuth Clients should a user see? This is a functional question and here are the specs:<div><ul><li>all clients in their org, if they have the "user" role</li><li>all clients to which the users org is subscribed.</li></ul></div>" style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=left;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;" vertex="1" parent="1"> <mxGeometry x="870" y="-690" width="350" height="140" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-48" style="rounded=0;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;orthogonalLoop=1;jettySize=auto;html=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;endArrow=none;startFill=0;strokeColor=default;dashed=1;" edge="1" parent="1" source="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-47" target="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-5"> <mxGeometry relative="1" as="geometry"> <mxPoint x="690" y="-443" as="sourcePoint" /> <mxPoint x="610" y="-442" as="targetPoint" /> </mxGeometry> </mxCell> </root> </mxGraphModel> </diagram> </mxfile>

div.div: TODO which accounts should which users be able to see? is it related to all those in the current org? or is it that if you are an account manager, you can see all accounts? no that ain't right because I don't want Joe Bloggs to see all my abstratium employees. Currently it's implemented that it fetches users that have roles in the clients that i have access too, but that is total BS.

switch.text: TODO which accounts should which users be able to see...

div.div:
TODO this is a functional question that i have to specify the answer to. built the solution based on those specs.
- ADMIN can see everyone. that's why no one gets it.
- an org owner can see all the accounts that are members in the given org.
- an org member can see other accounts in the org.
- the role "abstratium-abstrauth_manage-accounts' is given to anyone, but they can only manage accounts that have membership to their org.
- an org owner can add anyone to their org
- but then we have a security issue, since Joe Bloggs can add abstratium.dev@gmail.com to their org, and they would be able to manage my roles in abstrauth!
- equally, Joe Bloggs must be able to give anyone the role "abstratium-abstratuth_manage-accounts" within their org.
- is it as simple as the client abstratium-abstrauth is super special? i don't think so because someone might build an app for which they let anyone manage the roles for it, inside abstrauth.

switch.text: TODO this is a functional question that i have to specify the answer to. built the solution based on those specs....

so all org owners are account managers by default.
WHEN A USER REGISTERS / IS REGISTERED, always give them the user role to abstrauth in the org of the user who is adding them.
WHEN THE TOKEN IS CREATED, only read for the orgId for which the token is being created.
